name: Marinated Hickory-Smoked Chuck Roast
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Beef chuck roast, 1 1/2 inches thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Pierce the roast with garlic cloves, inserting them evenly spaced throughout.
2. In a bowl, mix oil, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, salt, basil, pepper, and hot pepper sauce.
3. Place the meat in a large plastic bag and pour in the marinade mixture. Seal the bag.
4. Refrigerate the meat, turning occasionally to coat it well with the marinade, for 6-8 hours or overnight.
5. About an hour before cooking, soak hickory chips in enough water to cover; drain them.
6. Drain the meat and reserve the marinade. Pat excess moisture from the meat with paper towels.
7. Arrange slow coals around a drip pan. Add hickory chips to the coals.
8. Place the roast over the drip pan on the grill. Cover the grill.
9. Grill 25 minutes per side, turning occasionally and brushing with marinade as needed.
10. Continue grilling until the meat is cooked through. Season to taste before serving, removing the garlic.
